checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document without any invented headings, body text, or conclusions. AI-generated metadata such as the title is permitted per the instructions.
All phone numbers and codes present in the output (828-258-3939, 877-626-3198, 8186681, 445085031) match the source document exactly. No fabricated contact information was found.

The document consists of a single page containing an agenda and logistics. All major sections (Heading, Date, Location, Remote Access, Agenda) are represented in the output. Although a specific URL is missing within the WebEx section, the section itself is present.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output misses the primary WebEx meeting URL. In the source, the URL is provided immediately after the 'WebEx:' label. In the output, element 5 contains 'WebEx:' and element 7 contains the meeting number/passcode, but the intervening content (the URL) is completely absent. This is a significant omission as it removes the primary method for remote access to the meeting. Based on the reading order gap (5 to 7), this content was dropped during conversion.
checks.b_evidence_output: [The URL is missing from the elements list]
checks.b_evidence_location: WebEx section, between elements 5 and 7 (reading order gap)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the omitted URL, the text content accurately captures the source. Reformatting of the agenda items into a list structure and the use of newlines within paragraphs for phone numbers/addresses are considered acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: No text blocks or sections from the source document are duplicated in the output JSON. Each unique piece of information (Date, Location, Agenda items) appears exactly once in the sequence.

The heading hierarchy is correctly implemented. The document title 'Federal Interagency Committee' is assigned as Heading Level 1 (H1), and the 'Agenda:' section is assigned as Heading Level 2 (H2). This is a logical and accessible structure.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structural defects are possible.

The reading order follows the linear, top-to-bottom sequence of the source document. While there is a gap in the reading_order sequence (skipping 6), the relative flow of the remaining content remains logically sound and consistent with the source PDF.
The agenda items listed in the source are correctly converted into a structural 'list' element in the JSON. This preserves the semantic meaning of the agenda items as a set of related points.

A critical link (the WebEx join URL) present in the source PDF is entirely missing from the output JSON. The element sequence jumps from reading_order 5 ('WebEx:') to reading_order 7 ('Meeting Number...'), omitting the URL content that exists between them in the source document.
checks.b_evidence_output: The URL text and link are absent from the output; the reading order skips index 6.
checks.b_evidence_location: Between paragraph index 4 and paragraph index 5

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the same visual organization and flow as the source PDF. Information is presented in the same sequence: document title, meeting metadata (date, location, remote access details, WebEx info), and a chronologically ordered agenda. While some line breaks differ due to HTML's responsive nature (e.g., the phone number following the address on the same line) and some labels include literal markdown-style bolding syntax ('**'), the visual hierarchy and placement of information are consistent with the source. The agenda is rendered as a standard bulleted list which is a typical and acceptable conversion for list-like structures. Content is found in the same relative locations.
The source document contains no tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present.
The source document consists entirely of text and does not contain any meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. No image rendering errors are observed.

No body content (paragraphs, list items, or tables) is visually duplicated. The document title appearing at the top of the viewport ('Federal Interagency Committee Agenda20190516 68614Dc2') and again at the start of the content body ('Federal Interagency Committee') is an intentional accessibility feature for document identification and navigation landmarks, not a content duplication defect. All other information, including the contact details and individual agenda items, appears exactly once.
